item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted for the test proposal with children, because the reason for proposing the test in the first place was not explicitly and compellingly identified. It felt like an immersion-breaking LOLFoundation moment. Note that I see absolutely nothing wrong with the concept of an amoral Foundation that uses children as D-class fodder, but it should be clear what additional information or benefit the Foundation hopes to gain from the experiment or procedure. Otherwise it feels like a lame joke.

Also, this doesn't make sense:

Since this date, no new active phases have been observed, despite the presence of individuals overnight.

The site's being monitored, contained, and systematically tested by the Foundation, but there's no experiment log for these individuals who were present overnight? What, did these individuals slip past Foundation security and spend the night while the researchers took a holiday? I'm sorry to say, it honestly feels like the author got sick of writing and gave up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like the tale "The Foundation Eats Babies" is a good examination of a situation like this, and probably a good explanation of where they would get child D-Class personnel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Might be a necessary evil thing. We've had SCPs that affect only or primarily children (such as Bobble the Clown), and it's hard to get experimental data if your only test subjects aren't in the right age group.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see any possible reason for having children as D-Class though. I get that it could be a headcanon thing, but it doesn't seem like something the Ethics Committee would approve of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aren't d-class usually criminals and convicts? So how to we have 8 and 6 year old d-classes.

You kinda answered your own question there. If D-class are usually (implying "not always") convicted criminals, then the 6 and 8 year old D-class are not convicted criminals and were enrolled into the D-class program through some other means.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aren't d-class usually criminals and convicts? So how to we have 8 and 6 year old d-classes. Seems like they would be in juvy, if they are criminals. I recommend a 18 and 19 year old because they could be charged as adults

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The test logs kinda dragged for me, but I ended up upvoting because of how they managed to beat the system.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

(btw: If you change the island this is located on to something tropical, it will fit all three JamCon themes.)
